title: Follow handler fetches attacker-controlled actor URL without SSRF protection
description: In server.rs:421-433, the inbox Follow handler fetches the `actor` field from the incoming JSON body via `state.http_client.get(follower_actor)`. The `follower_actor` URL is fully attacker-controlled — it comes from the unauthenticated (signature verification is skipped per broadside-to5p.2) activity body. This is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vector: an attacker can POST a Follow with actor set to any URL (e.g., `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/` on AWS, or any internal service). The reqwest client follows redirects by default. Fix: validate that the actor URL uses https:// and that its host is not a private/loopback/link-local address before fetching. Consider a deny-list of RFC 1918 / RFC 5735 ranges, or use a dedicated SSRF-safe HTTP client library.
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
